response.justification: The candidate is a versatile senior engineer with genuine tooling interest — including direct IDE development experience on NI LabWindows/CVI and authoring test frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts) — but the core stack requirements are largely unmet. Java appears only peripherally, there is no Kotlin or Spring/enterprise-framework experience, and the last decade centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang rather than the JVM ecosystem. For a role demanding deep JVM understanding and Spring expertise, the gap is too wide despite strong general engineering craft.
strengths: Hands-on IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) and authored language tooling/test frameworks
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, and community teaching/workshops
Proven experience with complex, high-scale systems (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
concerns: No demonstrable Kotlin or Spring/enterprise-framework experience
Java exposure is minimal and dated; recent work is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not JVM
No evidence of working with build tools, application servers, or JVM internals at depth
